description_long: This is a vintage twin-lens reflex camera, likely a Soviet-era Lubitel-2, characterized by its boxy, rectangular body and two prominent circular lenses stacked vertically on the front. The camera is primarily black with metallic accents, and features a textured exterior for grip. The top section has a pop-up viewfinder hood with a textured pattern, and the front displays Cyrillic script above the lenses. The sides show winding knobs and a port, while the back has a red film window and metal latches. The camera's construction appears robust, with metal and plastic components, and it is designed to be handheld or mounted on a tripod. This type of camera would be a rare sight in modern urban environments, often seen as a collectible or used by photography enthusiasts.
description: A vintage black twin-lens reflex camera with a boxy body, two stacked lenses, and a pop-up viewfinder hood. It features metallic accents, textured grip, and Cyrillic branding on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large circular lenses, Cyrillic branding, and the main textured face of the camera.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the pop-up viewfinder hood, winding knobs, and a side port.
description_view_2: Rear view with the film window, metal latches, and the back panel of the camera.
description_view_3: Right side view highlighting the viewfinder hood, side knob, and the plain textured side panel.
